metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_4932
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure S13: Halving Efr3a copy number restored the decreased mEPSC frequency in CA1 pyramidal neurons and the upregulated paired-pulse facilitation (PPF) at the SC-CA1 synapse in APP/PS1 mice.
Figure 8M-R: selectively deleting Efr3a at the presynaptic site of the SC-CA1 synapse effectively rescues the reduced release probability (Pr) in APP/ PS1 mice.
Figure 9C&F: Blocking mGluR5 with MTEP or deleting Efr3a in CA3 significantly ameliorated Aβ-induced LTP impairment (Fig. 9C), accompanying a decreased PPF following LTP induction (Fig. 9F).

Given the effect of halving Efr3a copy number on Pr, LTP and PPF, the BP term "synaptic vesicle priming" was selected. However, of important notice is that these results were only shown in the PS1 mouse model and we infer that this function of Efr3a also applies to the wildtype situation.. Experimental description: "APP/PS1 double-transgenic, 5xFAD, M146V, Grik4-cre, and Camk2a-creERT2 mice were purchased from The Jackson Laboratory (Bar Harbor, ME)."

"The following commercially available antibodies were used: ..rabbit anti-Efr3a (HPA023402, Sigma-Aldrich, 1:1000), ..."
